topic: Local AI Model Serving Frameworks in 2026: vLLM vs TGI vs Ray Serve

competitors.title: vLLM vs TGI: LLM Serving Framework Comparison 2026
key_points: vLLM's PagedAttention KV cache reduces memory fragmentation, enabling higher throughput
TGI (Text Generation Inference) excels in Hugging Face ecosystem integration and easy auth for gated models
vLLM supports dynamic multi-LoRA serving, TGI only single LoRA per server
Docker image size: vLLM ~8 GB, TGI ~6 GB
Both support OpenAI-compatible API, tensor parallelism, and continuous batching
vLLM wins on maximum tokens/second throughput, TGI better for teams using HF Inference Endpoints

competitors.title: LLM Inference Servers Compared: vLLM vs TGI vs SGLang vs Triton (2026)
key_points: TGI entered maintenance mode in December 2025, Hugging Face recommends vLLM or SGLang for new deployments
vLLM remains production standard with 14-24x higher throughput than naive HuggingFace Transformers
SGLang's RadixAttention achieves 85-95% cache hit rates on multi-turn chat workloads
Triton + TensorRT‑LLM offers maximum NVIDIA‑optimized performance but with higher setup complexity
Benchmarks: SGLang leads batch inference on H100 with 16,215 tokens/sec vs vLLM's 12,553 tokens/sec
Decision matrix: vLLM for high-concurrency API, SGLang for multi‑turn chat/agents, Triton for enterprise multi‑model pipelines

competitors.title: vLLM compatibility — Ray 2.54.1
key_points: Ray Serve LLM provides OpenAI‑compatible API compatible with vLLM's engine arguments
Enables distributed deployment, autoscaling, multi‑model serving, and advanced routing atop vLLM
Supports vLLM features: embeddings, structured output, vision language models, reasoning models
Ray Serve adds production features: autoscaling, multi‑model serving, advanced routing
Allows switching between vllm serve and Ray Serve with no code changes while scaling horizontally

stats.fact: On‑premise LLM serving platforms market size reached $3.08 billion in 2025, grew to $3.81 billion in 2026
stats.source: The Business Research Company, On Premise LLM Serving Platforms Market Report 2026

stats.fact: Market projected to reach $9.03 billion by 2030 at a CAGR of 24.1%
stats.source: The Business Research Company, On Premise LLM Serving Platforms Market Report 2026

stats.fact: vLLM delivers 2‑4× higher tokens/second throughput than naive serving, with 85‑92% GPU utilization under high concurrency
SGLang outperforms vLLM by 29% on batch inference workloads (16,215 vs 12,553 tokens/sec) on H100

angles: Choosing between high‑throughput vLLM and enterprise‑orchestrated Ray Serve for production deployments
Future of TGI: maintenance mode and migration paths to vLLM/SGLang
Specialized frameworks: SGLang for multi‑turn conversations vs vLLM for high‑concurrency APIs
On‑premise vs cloud‑managed serving trade‑offs in 2026's tightening data‑privacy regulations
keywords: vLLM vs TGI vs Ray Serve
outline: Introduction: The rise of local AI model serving in 2026
Market overview: Size, growth, and key drivers (data privacy, AI infrastructure)
Framework deep‑dive: vLLM (PagedAttention, throughput), TGI (HF ecosystem, maintenance mode), Ray Serve (orchestration, scaling)
Performance benchmarks: Throughput, latency, GPU utilization across workloads
Use‑case recommendations: High‑concurrency APIs, multi‑turn chat, batch inference, enterprise pipelines
Future trends: Speculative decoding, multi‑modal serving, edge‑deployment frameworks
Conclusion: How to choose the right framework for your 2026 project
